packs.name: community.openwop-team.demo
packs.description: First community-namespace demo pack. Mixed pack — one trivial node (`uppercase`) plus one agent (`greeter`) — exercising both extension paths in a single tarball under the open-publish `community.*` tier. Manual Ed25519 signed (not Sigstore) to demonstrate the non-OIDC publishing path documented in registry-operations.md §Step 2. MIT-licensed for SPDX-diversity with the Apache-2.0 core packs.

packs.name: core.openwop.agent-examples
packs.description: First pure-agent pack — ships two minimal agent manifests with no node runtime. Validates the `agents[]` extension to the pack manifest (per agent-manifest.schema.json + RFC 0003). Echo agent inlines its system prompt; Summarizer references `prompts/summarizer.md` to demonstrate the `systemPromptRef` path. Both agents are stateless (memoryShape.longTerm: false). Vendor-neutral: no model is pinned — hosts resolve via their BYOK aiProviders surface.

packs.name: core.openwop.ai
packs.description: Spec-canonical AI-call pack. Three foundational nodes (chat-completion, structured-output, embeddings) that route through the host's BYOK aiProviders surface — the pack itself makes no HTTP calls. peerDependencies on `aiProviders.supported`. Side-effectful (declared) so the engine's Layer-2 invocation log caches responses keyed on (runId, nodeId, request-hash) for replay safety.

packs.name: core.openwop.data
packs.description: Spec-canonical data-utility pack. Five pure, replay-safe nodes covering the most common workflow-author needs: string-case transformation, string splitting, array filtering, array mapping (with template substitution), and shallow object merging. Zero npm deps. Runs on any Node 20+ host advertising the openwop-node-packs profile.

packs.name: core.openwop.examples
packs.description: Spec-canonical examples pack — three minimal nodes (echo, coin-flip, delay-with-progress) that exercise the core node-pack contract end-to-end. Zero npm deps; runs on any Node 20+ host advertising the openwop-node-packs profile. See docs/PACKS-MVP-PLAN.md.

packs.name: core.openwop.http
packs.description: Spec-canonical HTTP fetch node. Single typeId `core.openwop.http.fetch` with retry, exponential backoff, and deterministic Idempotency-Key derivation for replay safety. Authentication is the workflow author's concern — pass an `Authorization` header via the `headers` config field (literal token or resolved via the host's secret-injection mechanism). The pack itself declares no `requiresSecrets` so it runs on every host regardless of `secrets.supported`. Zero npm deps; uses Node 20's built-in fetch (undici).

packs.name: core.openwop.integration
packs.description: Spec-canonical integration pack — universal communication primitives that route through the host's provider-specific adapters. Phase 1 v1.0.0 ships email-send + slack-message (both communication; both side-effectful with engine Layer-2 cost-once caching via deterministic Idempotency-Key derivation). File-system, database-query, and GitHub-action nodes defer to future minors — each needs real connection-pool / credential abstractions in the host.

packs.name: core.openwop.mcp
packs.description: Spec-canonical MCP-operation pack. Four nodes for workflow-author-direct interaction with registered MCP servers — list-tools, invoke-tool, read-resource, server-status. Complements the implicit LLM-mediated tool-call path documented in spec/v1/mcp-integration.md by exposing the same MCP operations as first-class workflow nodes. Routes through the host's MCP client (ctx.mcp.* surface); the pack itself speaks no MCP protocol.

packs.name: core.openwop.triggers
packs.description: Workflow-trigger pack. Seven entry-point shapes — webhook, schedule, event, envelope, chatMessage, canvas, artifact — that hosts use to register workflows for external-event-driven dispatch. Trigger nodes are pass-through executors: they forward `ctx.triggerData` (set by the engine when the trigger fires) to outputs. The pack contributes the typed config + payload schemas + the runtime pass-through.

packs.name: vendor.openwop.rust-hello
packs.description: Reference WASM node pack proving RFC 0008 ABI v1 works end-to-end across an independent toolchain. One node type: vendor.openwop.rust-hello.greet.
